name: BLACK LÉOPARD®
category: Home > TEA > Tea family > Black tea > BLACK LÉOPARD
description: Southern Africa is a territory with nearly infinite natural resources and a climate structured by the rainy season between November and March. Local tea bushes have adapted to this unique Terroir and produce artisanal harvests rich in flavour. The young, freshly hand-picked leaves are quickly sent to the atelier to be crafted into this particular tea which is then delicately smoked with the leaves of guava trees that grow on the plantation. To enjoy throughout the day, hot or cold. Dried leaves : The long, obsidian black leaves release unique notes of smoked ebony accentuated by strong, malted aromas. Infused leaves : The colour of marron glacé, heart notes of tonka bean assemblage with hints of pink peppercorn. Liqueur : Lucid caramel brown. The smooth, smoky sweetness of the liqueur caresses the palate with notes of woody nougat scored with a glaze of creamy date. Round and flavourful for the afficionados.
origin: Japan Formosa China Ceylan Himalaya - Darjeeling In

description: This magnificent tea is perfumed with the flavour of jasmine flowers. The delicate and unique aroma envelope the tea with  magical freshness. In the heart of dense, subtropical forests in the province of Yunnan, near the city of Pu-Erh, the tea garden that produces this gem is located 1,500 meters in altitude and is blessed with a very rich forest soil. These hand-picked, tender Yin Zhen buds – meaning ‘silver needles’ – are flavoured with the delicate aroma of jasmine and make this tea an absolute masterpiece.

name: BRUME DE JASMIN®
category: Home > TEA > Tea family > White tea > BRUME DE JASMIN
description: The jade-coloured leaves, long, beautiful and speckled in brown accompany a large proportion of silver tips covered in downy white. The nose, heady with the fragrance of jasmine, is carried along by woody notes. The liqueur is clear and tinted orpiment. The tasting opens on an elegant and lively bouquet of fresh jasmine while an incomparable harmony of fruity and woody tones unfolds along its silky body. This composition, which marries the ardour of a sparkling flower to the softness of white tea, is clear and subtle Our jasmine tea is scented according to a time-honoured tradition. Plucked at dawn, thousands of delicate jasmine flowers are layered into the tea leaves and buds. The dry tea thus absorbs all of the heady, natural fragrance from the fresh flowers. This melding is repeated several times so the essence suffuses the heart of the tea. Between perfuming, the tea is dried again so that new, freshly plucked flowers may impart more aroma. After giving up all their delicious jasmine scent to the tea, the dried flowers are then carefully collected by hand, one by one. The all-natural jasmine tea is finally ready to be enjoyed. *non-binding photo

name: CASTLETON MOONLIGHT
category: Home > TEA > Tea family > White tea > CASTLETON MOONLIGHT
description: Nestled in the vicinity of Kurseong, the « City of the White Orchid », Castleton has been one of Darjeeling’s most famous gardens since 1885. Enjoying an exceptional terroir, the planter passionately refines his methods so as to perpetually improve the quality of these harvests. The tea bushes planted at the beginning of the golden age of tea are the subject of special attention in order to bring out their aromatic quintessence which makes for the remarkable identity of this excellent garden. An ode to the senses and to pleasure. The Moonlight tea collection draws its inspiration from the ancestral tradition of night picking, still practiced in the most prestigious gardens of Darjeeling. Under the silvery light of the full moon, pickers delicately harvest the buds and young shoots in the cool of the night. This exceptional method, inherited from practices related to cosmic cycles, allows for the complete preservation of the essential oils and subtle aromas of the leaves while benefiting from the lunar energetic influence on the plant's sap. Moonlight teas are distinguished by their unique character: a remarkable aromatic complexity, floral and fruity notes of incomparable finesse, and a natural sweetness that evokes the serenity of Himalayan nights. Each cup reveals the mystical soul of this exceptional harvest, offering a sensory experience of rare elegance. Dried leaves: Carefully plucked, the young leaves display shades of copper, contrasting with the silver moon reflections of the buds. Harvested and hand-crafted with extreme finesse, they evoke the grace of imperial silk fibres, testifying to the mastery of the planter’s ancestral savoir-faire. Infused leaves: Once infused, the leaves reveal themselves in all their majesty; large and slender, their integrity preserved, and evoking the precious patina of golden silk adorned with pink highlights. Crafted one by one, mixing leaves and buds, a subtle aromatic score expands notes of blond sugar cane blend with candied apricot. A noble and exquisite harmony, like the flowering orchards that dominate the Himalayan plantations. Liquor: Radiant, pale crystalline gold in colour, this nectar reveals elegant nuances almond dragée. The cup reveals a floral roundness of powdery rose, culminating in a finish as precious as a buttercup caressed by the morning mists of the peaks. Cold brew: 1 - place 15g of tea in 1 litre of spring water and refrigerate for approximately 12 hours. 2 - filter out the leaves then let the liqueur come to room temperature. 3 - best served in a large balloon glass, filled up to a third. "Cold brew", a slow infusion in cold water, allows for a further exploration of the infinite aromatic palette of this grand cru. The cold allows new fragrances to express themselves in a dazzling and utterly intoxicating way.

name: DÉSIR®
category: Home > TEA > Tea family > Green tea > DÉSIR
description: Its cube-like shape is perfect for admiring the theatrical performance of blossoming teas - bouquet, leaves , flowers - and its size is just right for steeping one ball of crafted tea such as "Fleur du Désir." An orange ribbon attached to the knob on the cover transforms the teapot into a most appealing object. This teapot (0.35l) is delivered with 100g of "Sculpted Teas" in a silkscreened and lacquered container. Teapot (height 6 in., width 7 in.) Before using your glass teapot for the first time, we recommend following these instructions: 1. Wash with a soft sponge and soapy water. 2. Rinse thoroughly and dry completely. To brew your tea: • Pour boiling water into your teapot and discard the water. • Place the required amount of tea in the filter provided. • Pour hot water over the tea to cover it and replace the lid to keep it warm. • Once the brewing time is up, remove the filter and serve. Glass preserves the aromas without altering them. To care for your teapot: • Regular care: clean with a soft sponge and soapy water, rinse and dry. • Occasional maintenance to remove tannin deposits inside: leave a mixture of very hot water and white vinegar, lemon juice, bicarbonate of soda or citric acid to act. Empty the teapot, rinse with clean water and dry. Repeat as many times as necessary. Avoid extreme thermal shocks.

name: FLEUR DE VOYAGE™
category: Home > TEA > Tea family > Green tea > FLEUR DE VOYAGE™
description: Grown on the mountainous slopes of Fujian, this costly green tea is delicately crafted by hand, leaf by leaf, then patiently scented with jasmine flowers from the finest harvests (between April and June). At the heart of the composition, there are two saffron-coloured marigold, known to mark the passing time (in nature it closes at night and opens when the sun is sufficiently high in the sky). The steeped tea is aromatic, flavourful, fresh, and sweet.

name: FLEUR D'ORIENT™
category: Home > TEA > Tea family > Green tea > FLEUR D'ORIENT™
description: Made from buds, this hand-crafted green tea is flavoured by long contact with jasmine: over several days, the flowers are mixed with the tea on four successive occasions lasting several hours, so that the tea leaves steadily absorb the fragrance (this technique was developed during the Ming dynasty). It is adorned with chrysanthemum flowers (the Chinese symbol of autumn), jasmine, and several everlasting flowers (representing love) that lend the clear, fragrant cup a bouquet of colours that sparkle like fireworks.

name: HUANG CHA
category: Home > TEA > Tea family > Yellow tea > HUANG CHA
description: Dried leaves: The tea leaves are rolled with an ancient and precise technique, resulting in beautifully fine, long and undulating needles. The satiny, amber tones of the young leaves recall precious wood; scattered amongst them are many buds shining like strands of rose gold. Infused leaves: They express a surprisingly complex fruity score, with dominant notes of guava jam and ripe medlar. Liqueur: The cup lights up with a majestic shade of orange, as bright as a marigold. The fine down of the buds, suspended in the infusion, gives the liqueur a unique satiny appearance. The heady and gourmet notes of roasted apple and apricot form a resolutely fruity score behind which a veil of lemon glaze tiptoes in the background, refreshing and unexpected. This unforgettable moment continues with an exceptional lingering in the mouth, thanks to a supple, round and silky liqueur. Imperial beverage.

name: JASMIN MANDARIN™
category: Home > TEA > Tea family > Green tea > JASMIN MANDARIN™
description: Plucked at dawn, thousands of delicate jasmine flowers are layered into the tea leaves and buds. The dry tea thus absorbs all of the heady, natural fragrance from the fresh flowers. This melding is repeated several times so the essence suffuses the heart of the tea. Between perfuming, the tea is dried again so that new, freshly plucked flowers may impart more aroma. After giving up all their delicious jasmine scent to the tea, the dried flowers are then carefully collected by hand, one by one. The all-natural jasmine tea is finally ready to be enjoyed.

name: MARGARET’S HOPE MOONLIGHT
category: Home > TEA > Tea family > White tea > MARGARET’S HOPE MOONLIGHT
description: Inaugurated in 1864, this magnificent garden full of romance rises to nearly 2,000m in altitude. Nourished by the pure flowing waters of two rivers, the centenary tea plants flourish along the steep sides of the valley facing the peaks of the Himalayas, surrounded by forests and wildflowers. One of the most celebrated and emblematic gardens of Darjeeling. The Moonlight tea collection draws its inspiration from the ancestral tradition of night picking, still practiced in the most prestigious gardens of Darjeeling. Under the silvery light of the full moon, pickers delicately harvest the buds and young shoots in the cool of the night. This exceptional method, inherited from practices related to cosmic cycles, allows for the complete preservation of the essential oils and subtle aromas of the leaves while benefiting from the lunar energetic influence on the plant's sap. Moonlight teas are distinguished by their unique character: a remarkable aromatic complexity, floral and fruity notes of incomparable finesse, and a natural sweetness that evokes the serenity of Himalayan nights. Each cup reveals the mystical soul of this exceptional harvest, offering a sensory experience of rare elegance. Dried leaves: The dry, golden leaves harbour silver buds, like pearls of dew sparkling with lunar reflections. Infused leaves: The leaves unfurl, deploying an infinite palette of glowing golden tones and revealing silver buds preserved in their integrity. The fragrance it exhales evokes blackcurrant buds in springtime and a delicately caramelised roasted pear. Liquor: The liqueur radiates a golden glow in all its splendour, shining like a liquid jewel, offering a silky bouquet of lily. The cup reveals then a delicious score of macadamia nut and fresh pistachio, culminating with a lingering note of vivacious candied ginger. The ensemble recalls the serenity of gardens gently touched by the morning mist of the Himalayas. Cold brew: 1 - place 15g of tea in 1 litre of spring water and refrigerate for approximately 12 hours. 2 - filter out the leaves then let the liqueur come to room temperature. 3 - best served in a large balloon glass, filled up to a third. "Cold brew", a slow infusion in cold water, allows for a further exploration of the infinite aromatic palette of this grand cru. The cold allows new fragrances to express themselves in a dazzling and utterly intoxicating way.

description: Sublime emerald powder sparkling with suave fragrance. Delicious brioche-like aromas are enhanced with an elegant note of white flowers. The round and silky liqueur floods the palate with floral nuances evoking stewed white melon and precious orchid honey. A finale of rare elegance recalls the smooth sweetness of macadamia nut. A richly umami imperial elixir. PREPARATION TIPS : Place about 1,5 g of Matcha tea in a bowl, then pour in 4 cl of hot water (80°C). Swiftly whip the tea for 30 seconds with a bamboo whisk, or chasen, then enjoy.

name: MATCHA YUZU
category: Home > TEA > Tea family > Green tea > MATCHA YUZU
description: In the hushed atmosphere of Shizuoka’s sun-drenched gardens, the tender shoots of three exceptional tea cultivars — Saemidori, Yabukita, and Samidori — are masterfully blended to reveal their very essence. For hours on end, stone mills trace slow, almost orbital circles over the sumptuous green leaves, gradually transforming them into a celestial emerald powder. The golden peel of Japan’s finest yuzus completes this creation, bringing radiant bursts of light to this jade-hued sky.From this bold union of emerald powder and dawn-kissed zest is born a vivid, luminous matcha, where the silky umami sweetness of tea harmonizes with the dazzling freshness of yuzu. A subtle alliance of earth and light, where the art of craftsmanship becomes a poetry of taste. Preparation: Place about 1.5 g of Matcha tea in a bowl, then pour in 4 cl of hot water (80°C). Swiftly whip the tea for 30 seconds with a bamboo whisk, or chasen, then enjoy.

name: NAMRING UPPER MOONLIGHT
category: Home > TEA > Tea family > White tea > NAMRING UPPER MOONLIGHT
description: The celebrated garden of Namring was created in 1855 by the British. Cultivated at 1,525 meters in altitude, this cru is harvested from an absolutely magnificent and prestigious parcel of land. Ample sunlight and pure water from the Himalayas provoke the formation of tasty buds and hence the crafting of this grand millesime. The remarkable character of Namring’s terroir and the admirable work of the planters associated with the unique savoir-faire of Mariage Frères give rise to a rich splendor of lunar velvety shades. The Moonlight tea collection draws its inspiration from the ancestral tradition of night picking, still practiced in the most prestigious gardens of Darjeeling. Under the silvery light of the full moon, pickers delicately harvest the buds and young shoots in the cool of the night. This exceptional method, inherited from practices related to cosmic cycles, allows for the complete preservation of the essential oils and subtle aromas of the leaves while benefiting from the lunar energetic influence on the plant's sap. Moonlight teas are distinguished by their unique character: a remarkable aromatic complexity, floral and fruity notes of incomparable finesse, and a natural sweetness that evokes the serenity of Himalayan nights. Each cup reveals the mystical soul of this exceptional harvest, offering a sensory experience of rare elegance. Dried leaves: Preserved in their natural integrity, these whole leaves and buds are the fruit of a precious and delicate harvest conserving their celadon hue and pearly reflections. The buds, true silver needles, undulate gracefully among the most beautiful leaves in the world in a chromatic symphony of bright green. Infused leaves: The leaves display a soft green shade accented by young buds with golden glimmers, evoking the precious gold and silver-threaded embroidery of a teapicker's sari. The aroma that emerges subtly blends the delicacy of acacia with the freshness of white muscatel grape jelly, recalling the scents that rise from the terraced gardens caressed by the first morning breezes. Liquor: The liqueur reveals itself in a white amber robe, brilliant and clear like precious crystal. Its fresh and subtle bouquet is nuanced by the velvety sweetness of fresh sugar cane and hints of golden madeleine. On the palate, its round texture reveals flavours of pine nut. From leaf to cup, this tea transmits the magic of celestial dew! Cold brew: 1 - place 15g of tea in 1 litre of spring water; keep in the fridge for about 12 hours. 2 - filter out the leaves then let the liqueur come to room temperature. 3 - best served in a large balloon glass, filled up to a third. " Cold brew ", a slow infusion in cold water, results in a further exploration of the infinite aromatic palette of this grand cru. The cold allows new fragrances to express themselves in a dazzling and totally intoxicating way.

description: To prepare up to 24 litres of cold brew iced tea In the heart of Paris, summer is settling in; the lights are dancing on the cobblestones while people are strolling along the Seine, enjoying summer’s mild caress. This bright and romantic city, which saw the birth of Maison Mariage Frères on the 1st of June 1854, is at the heart of Paris Summer iced tea. On the black glass bottle blue, white and red ribbons undulate as they float along the Seine. The Eiffel Tower shines its bright light on the welcoming city below, buzzing with energy and joy. PREPARATION: To prepare up to 24 litres of cold brew iced tea - COLD BREW MINIMUM 1 H Simple preparation method in 3 easy steps, comes with a pack of Enveloppes Filtre® : 1 - Put 1 cap full (10g) of tea in 1 Enveloppe Filtre® 2 - Close by folding over the sides of the Enveloppe Filtre® 3 - In 1 to 1,5 litre of spring water, leave it in the refrigerator for at least 1 hour.

description: This ‘gold’ Pu-Erh was the authentic, precious tea of Chinese tribes under the Song and Qing dynasties. Only the young buds are chosen, carefully plucked early in the year (February), before the rainy season starts, when they are still fine and covered with down. With its elegant, golden leaves, this rare Pu-Erh is never packaged as a compressed tea. Its liqueur is deep red, dense yet mild with a scent of lotus and a slightly sweet taste. Aromatic and aged, it represents the height of refinement. It also boasts beneficial and soothing qualities.
origin: Japan Formosa China Ceylan Himalaya - Darjeeling In

name: PU-ERH, Millésime 2008
category: Home > TEA > Tea family > Black tea > PU-ERH, Millésime 2008
description: Just like the rarest Champagnes, the best harvests issued from specially reserved parcels of land are graded making this unique cru worthy of its place in our one-of-a-kind Pu-Erh collection. Dried leaves: The ashy, ebony leaves exhale a fresh fragrance of wild mallow and wet volcanic rock. Infused leaves: An exquisite bouquet of ginger torch assemblages with horn of plenty mushroom Liqueur: Shimmering wenge. Delicately earthy undertones yield to nuances of candied cherry coated in raw cocoa. The connoisseur’s delight.

name: PU-ERH SUPRÊME
category: Home > TEA > Tea family > Black tea > PU-ERH SUPRÊME
description: A privileged relationship with the planter has brought forth a ‘Suprême’ harvest and a meticulous imperial tea. Like the rarest of Cognacs, the best harvests issued from reserved parcels are married together to create the jewel of the already unique and extravagant collection of Pu-Erh available on the Mariage Frères tea menu. Dried leaves:The young leaves, elegant and golden, release a profusion of noble fragrances evoking black truffle wet with morning dew, the flowery-scented wood of the cocoa tree, and roasted chestnut. Infused leaves: Blazing amber. The infused leaves evoke a reverie of a forest walk surging with the aromas of autumn, joined by captivating notes recalling an antique mahogany humidor and a dash of musky Cologne. Liqueur Pale gold. The infusion is delicate, dense and rich with a myriad of fabulously seductive and enchanting notes. The graceful prelude of a bouquet of lotus, persistent and perfumed, is followed by a sweet and subtle dash of cinnamon. A frank and layered heart note of fern and iris diffuse with vanilla and tonka bean before ascending in a soft, zesty finale. Heavenly and virtuous.
origin: Japan Formosa China Ceylan Himalaya - Darjeeling In

name: PU-ERH, Millésime 2013
category: Home > TEA > Tea family > Pu-erh > PU-ERH, Millésime 2013
description: Dried leaves: Thick and undulating, the leaves unfold like ancient script, each curve echoing the grace of forgotten ideograms. Their surface is lightly dusted with rich, shadowy tones reminiscent of Macassar wood. Infused leaves: Broad swirls rise with solemn elegance, sketching fragrant traces of rare woods, timeworn leather, and golden beeswax—a silent symphony of scent. Liquor: The lip of the cup catches fleeting rays of amber light before they dissolve into the depths of a liquor cloaked in a robe as dark as Chinese ink. The tannins, smooth and enveloping, reveal delicate whispers of forest floor at dawn, resonating with the indulgent warmth of dark caramel infused with tonka bean. A tale both genuine and gourmand, inscribed in the very soul of the infusion.

name: SUMMER ROSÉ®
category: Home > TEA > Tea family > White tea > SUMMER ROSÉ
description: To prepare up to 24 litres of cold brew iced tea Here’s the checklist for a perfect summer: a glass, some ice cubes, and a carafe of  'SUMMER ROSÉ' – an iced white tea with sunny notes of red fruits and grapes unfolding around the vibrant accents of bergamot and pomegranate. Whether you’re tanning poolside, lounging on a heavenly beach, relaxing on the patio, or lazing in the grass with friends, this new iced tea delivers invigorating taste with plenty of joyful moments to share. The infusion is as intensely pink as a summer sunset, a perfect balance of freshness and indulgence. Under a silver wave, the handblown glass bottle is adorned with dazzling fuchsia droplets ready to spritz us with a cool blast of playfulness. - Shiny black hand-blown glass with measuring cap (h 175 mm – Ø 85 mm) - 160g of  SUMMER ROSÉ, good for up to 24 litres of iced tea - 1 pack of Enveloppes Filtre™ PREPARATION: To prepare up to 24 litres of cold brew iced tea - COLD BREW MINIMUM 1 H Simple preparation method in 3 easy steps, comes with a pack of Enveloppes Filtre® : 1 - Put 1 cap full (10g) of tea in 1 Enveloppe Filtre® 2 - Close by folding over the sides of the Enveloppe Filtre® 3 - In 1 to 1,5 litre of spring water, leave it in the refrigerator for at least 1 hour.

name: SWEETHEART®
category: Home > TEA > Tea family > Green tea > SWEETHEART
description: Before using your glass teapot for the first time, we recommend following these instructions: 1. Wash with a soft sponge and soapy water. 2. Rinse thoroughly and dry completely. To brew your tea: • Pour boiling water into your teapot and discard the water. • Place the required amount of tea in the filter provided. • Pour hot water over the tea to cover it and replace the lid to keep it warm. • Once the brewing time is up, remove the filter and serve. Glass preserves the aromas without altering them. To care for your teapot: • Regular care: clean with a soft sponge and soapy water, rinse and dry. • Occasional maintenance to remove tannin deposits inside: leave a mixture of very hot water and white vinegar, lemon juice, bicarbonate of soda or citric acid to act. Empty the teapot, rinse with clean water and dry. Repeat as many times as necessary. Avoid extreme thermal shocks.

name: MAORI BLUE, MILLESIME 2010
category: Home > TEA > Tea family > Blue tea > MAORI BLUE, MILLESIME 2010
description: Located in the district of Waikato (Maori for“flowing water”) — named after the largest river in the country and known for its ecological diversity with its green hills, caves, forests and lakes — a small tea estate on North Island has devoted itself to producing 100% Jardin Premier tea. This faraway grand cru is unique and magnificent. Dried leaves: The sumptuous whole leaves are like precious gems. Meticulously hand-crafted and rolled one by one, the light hugs their lustrous surface, coloured with opulent reflections of antique gold. Infused leaves: The first wisps of candied tamarind are followed by the sweet, fruity notes of manuka honey, incredibly decadent. The last fragrance evokes the delicacy of almond nougat. Liquor: The heady scent of magnolia flowers overruns the cup, harmoniously accented with notes of wild orchid and royal jelly. Lush in texture and sparkling gold in colour, its delicate milky aroma persists tirelessly on the palate, silky and delectable. Pearls of the Pacific. Tea in a Mariage Frères black pouch. AGED TEA® COLLECTION Each tea contains a part of this magical and ancestral history, where the terroir and the savoir-faire of the planters unite in perfect harmony. Entrusted to time’s loving embrace, the Aged Tea® Collection is perpetually getting better, improving and rounding out delicately and elegantly. As time passes, new notes are revealed, endlessly renewing the pleasure of taste. In this unique collection of Aged Teas®, Mariage Frères has brought together the most beautiful treasures from all of its prestigious terroirs and grand estates, offering a most delicious journey through time.

name: TANKA CHA™
category: Home > TEA > Tea family > Green tea > TANKA CHA™
description: Grown in the Kyoto Imperial region, this tea is a marriage between a refined sencha tea, well known for its subtle aroma and flavour and a Matcha tea, a fine powder made from the same fresh leaves used to obtain the prestigious Gyokuro, shaded from the sun before the plucking. Both are from Uji. Its leaves – of a jade green hue, elegant and regular – yield colour and brightness to the transparent liqueur. The cup, a rare balance of strength and delicacy, produces a rich, smooth liqueur and a flowery bouquet, fresh and sparkling with a slightly sweet and persistent note. This modern Tanka, derived from the eminent form of Japanese literature, is poetry to be leafed through ever so slowly.

name: THÉ DES MANDARINS JASMIN®
category: Home > TEA > Tea family > White tea > THÉ DES MANDARINS JASMIN
description: The prerogative of Mandarins. Our jasmine tea is scented according to a time-honoured tradition. Plucked at dawn, thousands of delicate jasmine flowers are layered into the tea leaves and buds. The dry tea thus absorbs all of the heady, natural fragrance from the fresh flowers. This melding is repeated several times so the essence suffuses the heart of the tea. Between perfuming, the tea is dried again so that new, freshly plucked flowers may impart more aroma. After giving up all their delicious jasmine scent to the tea, the dried flowers are then carefully collected by hand, one by one. The all-natural jasmine tea is finally ready to be enjoyed. *non-binding photo

description: To prepare up to 24 litres of cold brew iced tea For those days with sunny skies Mariage Frères has devised this new refreshing tea. It is specially conceived to make iced tea, and can be steeped in cold water using our own envelope-filters . The whole-leaf green tea is enlivened by leaves of mint and petals of rose. Its brilliant liqueur delivers a delightful grassy flavour that envelopes the palate and is countered by fresh, aromatic notes of mint, inflected by sparkling hints of rose. A bouquet of sensations in the mouth. PREPARATION: To prepare up to 24 litres of cold brew iced tea - COLD BREW MINIMUM 1 H Simple preparation method in 3 easy steps, comes with a pack of Enveloppes Filtre® : 1 - Put 1 cap full (10g) of tea in 1 Enveloppe Filtre® 2 - Close by folding over the sides of the Enveloppe Filtre® 3 - In 1 to 1,5 litre of spring water, leave it in the refrigerator for at least 1 hour.

description: The plum blossom, a timeless symbol of hope and renewal, braves winter’s icy grip to unfurl its delicate buds. Blooming beneath the season’s final snowflakes, it gracefully announces the reawakening of nature. To celebrate this annual wonder, the distinguished Maison MARIAGE FRÈRES proudly presents the latest vintage in its esteemed UME, UME!® collection of teas, imbued with the delicate fragrance of blooming plum trees: The exceptional 'UME CHA 2025' green tea brings a touch of brilliance to winter with its airy floral bouquet. Its gentle infusion reveals a refined interplay of floral and fruity nuances, offering a truly sublime experience. Presented in a dazzling white canister adorned with a cascade of golden snowflakes, this tea is a masterpiece of elegance. The accompanying case, rendered partly in a tender green evoking the first buds breaking under the snow, is adorned with a striking crimson plum blossom, whose sensual beauty attracts joyous swallows. Together, they proclaim the arrival of brighter and more splendid days ahead.

description: The plum trees bloom beneath the snow, like a glimmer of light in the heart of winter, symbolising hope and renewal. description: The hinterland of Provence in southern France explodes with plants, flowers, and fruit that all boast heady scents. Lavender, rosemary, thyme, broom, juniper, heather, and boxwood dot hills and plateaus; forests of silvery mimosa colour the mountains of Maures and Estérel, and roses, tulips, jasmine, and violets blanket Grasse; finally, golden fruit trees (lemon, orange) light up the Riviera. In order to recreate the magic of southern France, we had a large green tea with a flowery, slightly herbaceous fragrance, and assemblageed it with fruits from Provence, dominated by ripe red and black berries, creating an intense bouquet that is round in the mouth, concentrated in flavour, supported by the warm aroma of mellow lavender and the sweetness of rose petals.

name: YEN BAI PU-ERH 2007®
category: Home > TEA > Tea family > Pu-erh > YEN BAI PU-ERH 2007
description: In the north of Viêt-Nam, the province of Yen Bai is famous for its breathtaking landscapes featuring green mountains sculpted by rice fields whose terraces hug the winding hills. It is also the cradle of many indigenous communities for whom traditions are deeply rooted in daily life. Since time immemorial, the native peoples have been picking the leaves of centuries-old wild tea trees rooted in the fertile soil of the misty mountains. An authentic matured tea, a return to the land and to nature. Dried leaves: Long and splendid whole leaves whose slow maturation imparts a brown shade of colour like snakewood. Infused leaves: Notes of oak and moss transport you to a forest after the rain, mingling with the aroma of banana leaf. Liqueur: The infusion reveals a shade of mahogany with sublime garnet reflections. Its concentrated scent evokes an aroma of forest floor misted with dew, spiced with a hint of delicately crumpled pepper leaf. The lively liqueur caresses the palate and evolves towards a gourmet, fruity note of sapodilla and chestnut cream. Presented in a Mariage Frères black pouch.

name: YIN ZHEN
category: Home > TEA > Tea family > White tea > YIN ZHEN
description: « Silver Needles », the height of perfection in white tea. The so-called ‘imperial’ plucking is done exclusively by hand on just two days of the year, when only the finest young buds are selected. The marvellous leaves of Yin Zhen resemble needles covered by silvery tips, and yield a crystalline, pale mandarin liqueur with the subtle, fresh fragrance of buds. It is the most glamorous and costly white tea on earth.
